mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO is an always-on production trend signal: expanding factory output raises near-term revenue expectations and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional hit rate, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is above 9.0% and does not decline over three months, persistent labor-market slack signals weak household income growth and fragile demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.0% and 3-month change is >= 0.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered mean returns are not below non-triggered mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and INDPRO is rising over three months, zero-rate liquidity combines with improving real activity to compress discount rates and lift expected earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has no higher 60-day mean return than periods with only one condition active, or if interaction-term coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below 600 thousand units signals a housing-sector demand shortfall that reduces construction activity, durable-goods spending, and bank credit quality, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 episodes have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or do not underperform HOUST-above-600 episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in RSAFS is an always-on consumption momentum signal: stronger retail sales increase expected corporate revenues and analyst earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes fail to predict higher 5-day returns than negative changes, or if the sign hit rate is not above 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above a 3% annualized 6-month pace while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, rising inflation pressure at the policy lower bound raises risk-premium and margin concerns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above 3% and FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have non-negative average 20-day excess returns versus baseline or if the CPIAUCSL-by-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS 3-month net job growth crosses above 200 thousand, accelerating employment signals rising wage income and consumption breadth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change crosses above +200 thousand jobs
failure_condition: Reject if triggered PAYEMS accelerations have positive 20-day returns in no more than the unconditional rate or if their average return is not statistically above non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 grows quarter over quarter while UNRATE remains above 8%, the economy is in an early recovery with spare labor capacity and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with high labor slack
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-growth and high-UNRATE condition does not outperform periods with GDPC1 growth and UNRATE <= 8%, or if 60-day hit rate is below 55%.
